title: Al Ittihad Road @ Al Mamzar (Dubai–Sharjah border)

triage.narrative: Al Ittihad Road at Al Mamzar (Dubai–Sharjah border) is the RTA’s worst-performing corridor-junction pair, with 8.27% of hours at gridlock (LOS F) and an unmet demand of 117.7 vehicles per hour. The mean volume-to-capacity ratio of 0.498 masks severe peak-hour oversaturation, as the junction logs 315 phase failures in three years—the highest of any signal in the network.

diagnosis.summary: The problem is a fixed-time signal at JCT_MAMZ, which is failing 315 times in three years and creating a demand gap of 117.7 vph. The junction is only 0.515 km from the corridor, so its failures directly gridlock the border-bound lanes of Al Ittihad Road.
evidence: 315 phase failures over 3 years (2023-2025) — worst of 10 junctions
Demand gap 117.7 vph (mean demand_vph − mean volume_vph over 26,304 hours)
diagnosis._confidence_basis: Deterministic: 40 + 15×n_supporting_signals − 10×indirect_link, clamped to 95. n=4 (severe congestion los_f 8.27%>4; worst-stressed co-located signal 315 phase failures; tight co-location 0.515 km; Fixed-time = directly retimable cause), indirect=0 → 40+60=100 → clamp 95.

simulate._comment: Before-state grounded in observed top-decile-saturation hours at JCT_MAMZ (deg_sat 0.763, avg delay 36.4 s/veh; AM-Peak program C=140s, through green 41s). vc = junction degree of saturation (the quantity the fix changes; distinct from the corridor's 3-yr mean_vc). After-delay via HCM/Webster control delay recomputed at the reallocated green split, scaled from the observed before value by the formula ratio. Indicative, not micro-simulated. Scores on a 1-5 scale — cost: 1=config/software, 2=minor works/ops, 3=moderate capital (detectors/controller), 4=significant capital (lane), 5=major infrastructure; disruption: 1=none/overnight, 2=minor/short closures, 3=peak-hour lane closures, 4=extended construction, 5=long-term major works.

candidates.fix: Retime JCT_MAMZ within existing green bounds (reallocate green from the left-turn phases to the critical through movement, cycle length unchanged)
Upgrade JCT_MAMZ from Fixed-time to adaptive (SCOOT) control
candidates._comment: After-state models near-optimal green allocation (through green pushed toward its 66s max bound). High uncertainty / optimistic bound: the adaptive junction JCT_DEF at similar demand runs only ~5% lower delay than JCT_MAMZ and still logs 130 phase failures.

rank.recommendation: Retime JCT_MAMZ within its existing green bounds, shifting time from left-turn phases to the critical through movement. Indicative results: junction delay drops from 36.4 to 29.1 seconds per vehicle, and degree of saturation improves from 0.763 to 0.626. Cost and disruption are minimal (scores 1/1).
rank.top_fix: Retime JCT_MAMZ within existing green bounds

flow_animation._comment: Grounded in observed mean corridor speeds by Level-of-Service band for ITT_W1 (ff=80 kph): before = LOS-F band (worst recurring peak), after = LOS-D band (target after the top fix). speed_factor = mean band speed / free-flow speed.
